title: Yang automation–distribution coordination break
summary: Automation raises productivity but the wage-based distribution mechanism ties income to jobs. As displacement eliminates jobs, income collapses even as abundance grows. UBI is modeled as a latent distribution channel that would re-couple capability to human security.
source: Ch09 §9.2 — Yang interview; "productivity increases, wages decrease; wealth increases, but inequality rises."

nodes.label: Automation Capability
nodes.description: AI and robots replacing human labor

nodes.label: Economic Security
nodes.description: Ability to meet needs, pursue purpose

identifiability_note: UBI (n5) is the unobserved distribution channel; the net effect of automation on security depends on whether this latent channel is activated.
